title: Content-based features for cold-start recommendation
text: Cold-start recommendation often relies on side information such as item metadata, text embeddings, and user profile features. Content-based models can recommend new items before enough interaction data exists, but they may underperform collaborative methods once interaction histories become rich.

title: Popularity and priors in cold-start systems
text: A simple cold-start baseline is to recommend globally popular or trending items. This approach is robust and easy to deploy, but it can overexpose head items and reduce personalization and catalog coverage.

title: Collaborative filtering strengths and limitations
text: Collaborative filtering uses user-item interaction patterns to infer preferences. It often performs well with dense behavioral data, but it struggles when new users or items have little to no history.

title: Reranking in recommendation pipelines
text: Modern recommendation systems often use multi-stage ranking. Retrieval generates a candidate set, then a reranker optimizes a richer objective using more expensive features. Better reranking can improve quality without changing agent planning.
